summary: express-session-kit typosquats express-session and executes remote code on require()
details: Package impersonates the widely-used express-session (identical author attribution, README, copyright headers) under a confusable name and adds a load-time remote-code-execution loader. On require(), index.js invokes initServer() at module top level, spawning a detached node child (stdio:'ignore', child.unref()) to run session/check.js so the loader persists after require() returns. session/check.js performs an axios GET to a hardcoded, mutable non-publisher endpoint (http://check-server-state.vercel.app/server/v2) over plain HTTP with a static 'bearrtoken: gemini' header; on an HTTP 404 response containing a token field, the response body is passed to new Function('require', body) and invoked with the live require reference, giving the remote server arbitrary code execution in the installing process. session/config.js fragments the URL, path, version and header value across four exported constants (API_SERVER_DOMAIN, sub, token, VERIFY_TOKEN) to hide the C2 from simple string scanners, and the header name is deliberately misspelled. None of this behavior is related to the advertised session-middleware purpose.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
